hypothesis: Under the coarse-grained model at fixed strong-bead fractions and interaction strengths, the magnitude of the binding free energy will exhibit a maximum when the surface heterogeneity correlation length equals the average spacing of strong monomers along the polymer (Ls ≈ Lp), with at least a 20% reduction in |ΔF| when Ls/Lp ≥ 1.5 or ≤ 0.67.
